title: Pithecellobium ferdinandi-muelleri
taxonomy: Pithecellobium ferdinandi-muelleri is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and is commonly known as the 'black bean tree'. The classification of this tree includes its genus, which is a part of the subfamily Mimosoideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Pithecellobium ferdinandi-muelleri is a legume tree, which means it produces seed pods containing seeds that are high in protein.
uses: Pithecellobium ferdinandi-muelleri is a tree species native to Australia, and it has been found to have some traditional medicinal uses in the country.
